_comment: Flavor text for Sigh's Hit Attribution module. Each key is a layer type, each sub-key is a damage type (or 'default'). Values are arrays — one line is chosen at random. Tokens: {attacker} {attack} {defender} {armorName} {shieldName} {spellName} {dexMod} {abilityMod} {abilityAbbr}

default: {attacker}'s {attack} goes hopelessly wide — it never had a chance.
{attacker} stumbles through the {attack}, missing by a mile.
A clumsy {attack} from {attacker} connects with nothing but air.
{attacker} overextends on the {attack} and nearly loses their footing entirely.
piercing: {attacker}'s {attack} goes wide, finding nothing but empty air.
The {attack} from {attacker} is telegraphed a mile away — {defender} barely needed to move.
{attacker} lunges with {attack} but the angle is all wrong from the start.
slashing: {attacker}'s {attack} sweeps through empty air with an embarrassing whoosh.
The {attack} from {attacker} is wild and uncontrolled — it was never going to land.
{attacker} over-rotates on the {attack} and stumbles past {defender} entirely.
bludgeoning: {attacker} brings the {attack} down hard — onto nothing.
The {attack} from {attacker} crashes into the ground well short of {defender}.
{attacker} winds up for a massive {attack}... and completely whiffs.
fire: {attacker}'s {attack} dissipates harmlessly before reaching {defender}.
The flames from {attacker}'s {attack} gutter and die in the air between them.
A weak {attack} from {attacker} fizzles out well short of its mark.
cold: {attacker}'s {attack} scatters on the wind before reaching {defender}.
The chill of {attacker}'s {attack} disperses into nothing — far too weak to matter.
Ice crystals from the {attack} fall short, {attacker}'s aim completely off.
lightning: {attacker}'s {attack} sparks and arcs away into the ground, hitting nothing.
The {attack} from {attacker} forks wildly and grounds itself well clear of {defender}.
A crackling {attack} fizzles in {attacker}'s hands before it can even form properly.
thunder: {attacker}'s {attack} rolls off in entirely the wrong direction.
The shockwave from {attacker}'s {attack} dissipates before it reaches {defender}.
{attacker}'s {attack} lets loose a thunderous blast — that hits nothing.
acid: {attacker}'s {attack} splatters the ground well short of {defender}.
A glob of corrosive fluid from {attacker}'s {attack} lands nowhere near its target.
poison: The {attack} from {attacker} goes wide, the poison serving no purpose.
{attacker} lunges with {attack} and hits absolutely nothing.
necrotic: {attacker}'s {attack} bleeds into the ground, touching nothing living.
The {attack} from {attacker} passes harmlessly through empty space.
{attacker}'s {attack} unravels before it can find a target.
radiant: {attacker}'s {attack} blooms in the wrong direction entirely.
The {attack} from {attacker} flares and fades, having touched nothing.
{attacker}'s {attack} blazes wide and illuminates only empty floor.
force: {attacker}'s {attack} streaks past {defender} and dissipates into the wall.
The {attack} from {attacker} shunts through empty air.
{attacker}'s {attack} surges in entirely the wrong direction.
psychic: The {attack} from {attacker} glances off nothing of consequence.

default: The {attack} lands squarely but {defender}'s {armorName} turns it aside.
{attacker}'s {attack} connects — and stops dead against {defender}'s {armorName}.
{defender}'s {armorName} absorbs the hit. {defender} barely registers it.
A solid {attack} from {attacker}, but {defender}'s {armorName} does exactly what it was made to do.
piercing: The {attack} finds no gap in {defender}'s {armorName}, skittering off harmlessly.
{attacker}'s {attack} is stopped cold by {defender}'s {armorName} — no penetration.
The {attack} bites into {defender}'s {armorName} but fails to punch through.
slashing: The {attack} skitters across {defender}'s {armorName} with a harsh screech of metal.
{attacker}'s {attack} rakes across {defender}'s {armorName} and slides off without purchase.
A ringing {attack} against {defender}'s {armorName} — sparks fly, but no wound follows.
bludgeoning: {defender}'s {armorName} absorbs the {attack} with a resonant clang.
The {attack} hammers into {defender}'s {armorName} and transfers harmlessly.
{attacker} lands the {attack} but {defender}'s {armorName} spreads the force wide.
fire: Flames from the {attack} lick across {defender}'s {armorName} but fail to find flesh beneath.
The fire chars the surface of {defender}'s {armorName} but stops before reaching {defender}.
{defender}'s {armorName} smolders briefly under {attacker}'s {attack} — then nothing.
cold: Ice crystals from the {attack} form across {defender}'s {armorName} but the chill never reaches them.
Frost clings to {defender}'s {armorName}, slowing nothing, harming no one.
{attacker}'s {attack} coats {defender}'s {armorName} in a thin rime — and stops there.
lightning: Lightning from the {attack} crackles across {defender}'s {armorName} and grounds out.
The {attack} discharges against {defender}'s {armorName} in a shower of sparks.
{attacker}'s {attack} races across {defender}'s {armorName} and finds nowhere to go.
thunder: The shockwave of the {attack} hammers {defender}'s {armorName} but fails to breach it.
{defender}'s {armorName} rings like a bell from the {attack} — {defender} unhurt.
acid: Acid from the {attack} hisses across {defender}'s {armorName}, eating the surface but not through it.
{defender}'s {armorName} is pitted by the {attack} — but it holds.
Corrosive fluid from {attacker}'s {attack} splashes against {defender}'s {armorName} and eats no deeper.
poison: The venom from the {attack} coats {defender}'s {armorName} harmlessly — no way through.
{defender}'s {armorName} keeps the poisoned {attack} well away from their skin.
necrotic: Dark energy from the {attack} washes over {defender}'s {armorName} and dissipates.
{defender}'s {armorName} resists the necrotic surge — {defender} is unaffected.
The death energy bleeds away across {defender}'s {armorName}, spent without purpose.
radiant: Radiant light floods across {defender}'s {armorName}, bright but ultimately harmless.
The holy {attack} flares brilliantly against {defender}'s {armorName} and goes no further.
force: The force of the {attack} staggers {defender}'s {armorName} but transfers no real harm.
Raw force from the {attack} slams into {defender}'s {armorName} and fails to penetrate.
psychic: The {attack} ripples across {defender}'s {armorName}. Odd, but {defender} is unaffected.
{defender}'s {armorName} somehow grounds the {attack}. {defender} blinks, confused but unharmed.
Mental force washes over {defender}'s {armorName} — the armor's inert bulk immune to such things.

default: The {attack} glances off {defender}'s hardened draconic scales without leaving a mark.
{attacker}'s {attack} connects — but {defender}'s scales are tougher than they look.
Draconic heritage runs deep. That {attack} barely registered on {defender}'s hide.
The scales ripple under the {attack}, shifting with it. {attacker} hits nothing vital.
piercing: The {attack} finds no gap in {defender}'s draconic scales.
{attacker}'s {attack} slides off the interlocking scales — no entry.
Draconic scales are made for exactly this. The {attack} achieves nothing.
slashing: The {attack} rakes across {defender}'s scales with a sound like drawn steel — no wound.
{attacker}'s {attack} sparks across the draconic hide and accomplishes nothing.
Each scale deflects a little of the {attack}. Combined they deflect all of it.
bludgeoning: The scales flex under the {attack}, spreading it harmlessly.
{defender}'s draconic heritage turns the {attack} into a non-event.
{attacker}'s {attack} hammers against the scales. They give a little — just enough.
fire: Fire against draconic scales? {defender} practically yawns at the {attack}.
The {attack} washes over {defender}'s scales — this lineage was born in fire.
{attacker}'s {attack} splashes off {defender}'s draconic hide like water.
cold: Cold from the {attack} bites at {defender}'s scales but finds little purchase.
Frost from {attacker}'s {attack} settles across the draconic hide and melts away.
{defender}'s scales shed the {attack}'s chill like a second skin.
lightning: Lightning from the {attack} crackles across {defender}'s scales and arcs away harmlessly.
The {attack} dances across draconic hide and grounds out completely.
{defender}'s scales conduct the {attack}'s lightning away before it can do harm.
acid: Acid from the {attack} hisses across the scales — but draconic hide is used to worse.
The {attack}'s corrosive splash beads off {defender}'s scales like rain.
{defender}'s draconic lineage has made their hide resistant to far worse acid than this {attack}.

default: The {attack} strikes {defender}'s natural hide — tough enough to turn it aside.
{defender}'s natural armor absorbs the {attack} without complaint.
{attacker}'s {attack} lands on hardened natural hide and goes no further.
Nature gave {defender} their protection. It holds against the {attack}.
piercing: The {attack} presses against {defender}'s hide — and goes no further.
{attacker}'s {attack} is swallowed by {defender}'s thick natural armor.
Natural hide is deceptively tough. The {attack} can confirm this.
slashing: The {attack} skids across {defender}'s thick hide, barely leaving a scratch.
{attacker}'s {attack} opens no wound — the natural armor is too dense.
Thick hide sheds the {attack} like it was nothing.
bludgeoning: {defender}'s natural armor is built for exactly this kind of {attack}.
The {attack} thuds into {defender}'s hide and transfers nowhere useful.
Dense natural armor soaks the {attack} and gives {defender} nothing to worry about.
fire: Flames from the {attack} lick at {defender}'s hide but natural toughness keeps the heat at bay.
{defender}'s thick skin resists the {attack}'s fire better than expected.
The {attack} chars the outer layer of {defender}'s hide — nothing more.
cold: The {attack}'s cold bites but {defender}'s natural hide insulates well.
Frost from {attacker}'s {attack} settles across {defender}'s natural armor — a minor inconvenience.
{defender}'s thick natural hide keeps the {attack}'s chill from sinking in.
acid: Acid from the {attack} eats at the outer layer of {defender}'s hide — there's plenty more beneath.
{defender}'s natural armor is thick enough to lose a little to the {attack} and barely notice.
The {attack}'s corrosive splash is absorbed by {defender}'s outermost hide. It goes no deeper.

default: {defender} shifts just enough at the last moment — their reflexes pull them clear of the {attack}.
An almost imperceptible sidestep from {defender} is all it takes.
{attacker} had the right target. {defender}'s body just wasn't there when the {attack} arrived.
Quick feet and quicker instincts. {defender} wasn't where the {attack} expected.
piercing: {defender} twists at the hip — the {attack} passes through empty air where they just were.
The {attack} arrives on time. {defender} does not. It finds nothing.
A neat deflection of the body — {defender} angles away from the {attack} entirely.
slashing: {defender} leans back just far enough. The {attack} passes close enough to cut air.
The {attack} sweeps in — and {defender} is simply no longer in its path.
{defender} reads the angle of the {attack} early and steps cleanly outside it.
bludgeoning: {defender} ducks under the {attack} at the last instant — it whistles overhead.
Footwork saves {defender} here. The {attack} lands where they were, not where they are.
{defender} rolls with the angle of the {attack}, letting it pass harmlessly.
fire: {defender} moves fast enough that the {attack}'s flames trail behind them, finding nothing.
Quick reflexes carry {defender} clear of the {attack} before it can catch.
{defender} dances back out of the {attack}'s blaze before it can take hold.
cold: {defender} steps clear of the {attack}, the ice forming just behind their heel.
The {attack} races toward {defender} — who is already somewhere else.
Cold air from the {attack} rushes past {defender} as they dodge just ahead of the chill.
lightning: {defender} was already moving when the {attack} struck — a half-second too late.
The {attack} hits the spot {defender} just vacated. Instinct beat electricity.
{defender} drops low and the {attack} crackles through the space above them.
thunder: {defender} rolls clear of the {attack}'s shockwave before it can fully engulf them.
The {attack} sweeps past {defender} who was already moving laterally.
Reaction speed makes all the difference — {defender} is out of the {attack} before it arrives.
force: {defender} sidesteps the {attack} — felt it displace the air as it passed.
The {attack} hits nothing — {defender} was a half-step ahead of it.
The {attack} shunts through empty space where {defender} no longer stands.
psychic: {defender}'s mind flickers away from the {attack} before it can gain a foothold.
Awareness lets {defender} sense the {attack} coming — and slip aside.
The {attack} finds {defender}'s mind — but only its retreating edge.
necrotic: {defender} moves clear before the {attack}'s energy can find living tissue to unravel.
The {attack} chases {defender} and arrives a moment too late.
Deft footwork puts distance between {defender} and the {attack}.

default: {defender}'s battle-hardened body absorbs the {attack} — sheer constitution holds.
Years of punishment have built {defender} into something hard to hurt. The {attack} proves it.
The {attack} lands somewhere it should have mattered. {defender}'s constitution disagrees.
{attacker}'s {attack} hits flesh and bone toughened far beyond the norm. Not enough.
piercing: The {attack} penetrates — just not deep enough. {defender}'s dense muscle stops it cold.
{defender}'s constitution-built toughness swallows the {attack} before it reaches anything vital.
A lesser body would have fallen to that {attack}. {defender}'s does not.
slashing: The {attack} opens a line — and stops. {defender}'s constitution is as thick as their hide.
{attacker}'s {attack} bites in and finds nothing vital. Sheer toughness wins.
{defender} bleeds a little from the {attack}. Their body barely acknowledges it.
bludgeoning: The {attack} is bone-rattling. {defender}'s bones just don't rattle.
{attacker}'s {attack} hits something solid. Constitutionally speaking, it hits back harder.
The {attack} would shatter stone. {defender}'s constitution is apparently comparable.
fire: {defender} has taken {attack}s before. They've taken worse. They stand.
The {attack} finds purchase — briefly. Constitution builds resistance to more than weapons.
{defender} grits through the {attack}. It fades. They remain.
cold: {defender} shudders once from the {attack} — and shakes it off. Constitution runs deep.
The {attack}'s cold sinks in. {defender}'s constitution pushes it back out.
The {attack} bites at {defender}. They bite back harder.
necrotic: The {attack}'s necrotic energy bores into {defender}. Their constitution fights it to a standstill.
Life-draining {attack} meets a life-force too stubborn to drain easily.
{defender}'s constitution is not a subtle thing. It simply refuses the {attack}.

default: {defender} flows with preternatural calm — their focused mind sensed the {attack} coming.
Stillness and awareness are {defender}'s armor here. The {attack} finds neither opening.
{defender} doesn't dodge so much as simply not be where the {attack} expects.
There is no gap in {defender}'s awareness. The {attack} finds no gap in their defense.
piercing: {defender}'s mind was already a half-breath ahead. The {attack} finds nothing.
Focused awareness lets {defender} feel the {attack}'s angle before it arrives.
The {attack} reaches {defender} — who has already arranged themselves for it to miss.
slashing: {defender} reads the arc of the {attack} through something beyond instinct. They are not in it.
Still water reflects everything. {defender}'s calm mind reflects the {attack} — and avoids it.
The {attack} sweeps wide of a target that simply isn't there anymore.
psychic: {defender}'s disciplined mind closes like a fist around the {attack} — then expels it.
Mental training runs deep in {defender}. The {attack} finds nothing to grip.
{defender}'s inner stillness is a wall. The {attack} breaks on it.
force: Awareness of intent lets {defender} respond to the {attack} before it fully manifests.
{defender}'s trained mind perceives the {attack} and moves accordingly.
The {attack} arrives. {defender}'s wisdom already had them moving.

default: The {attack} strikes but {defender}'s natural constitution soaks up what their hide cannot.
{defender}'s thick frame absorbs what's left of the {attack} after the natural armor. Nothing useful remains.
Natural toughness, inside and out. {attacker}'s {attack} runs out of damage before running out of {defender}.
Body and hide working together — the {attack} finds neither vulnerable.
bludgeoning: Dense natural constitution spreads the {attack}'s impact. {defender} is built to take this.
The {attack}'s force travels through {defender}'s thick body and disperses without meaningful harm.
{defender}'s constitution-reinforced frame absorbs the {attack} like it was expected.
piercing: Hide slows it, constitution stops it. The {attack} never reaches anything it shouldn't.
Layers of toughness — the {attack} runs out of momentum before {defender} runs out of body.
{defender}'s natural mass stops the {attack} cold.

default: {defender} raises {defender}'s {shieldName} just in time — the {attack} glances off with a clang.
{defender}'s {shieldName} is in the right place at the right moment. The {attack} goes nowhere.
{defender} reads the {attack} and interposes {defender}'s {shieldName}. Problem solved.
The {attack} lands on {defender}'s {shieldName} with a satisfying clang. {defender} is entirely unbothered.
piercing: The {attack} finds {defender}'s {shieldName} instead of flesh. It stops there.
{defender} angles {defender}'s {shieldName} to catch the {attack} — it skitters off harmlessly.
A well-placed {shieldName} and the {attack} is completely neutralized.
slashing: The {attack} rings off {defender}'s {shieldName} in a shower of sparks.
{defender} takes the {attack} on {defender}'s {shieldName} and gives nothing back.
Metal on metal — {defender}'s {shieldName} wins this exchange convincingly.
bludgeoning: {defender}'s {shieldName} absorbs the {attack}'s force. {defender}'s arm aches — nothing more.
The {attack} hammers {defender}'s {shieldName} back. They brace. They hold.
{defender} plants their feet and takes the {attack} on {defender}'s {shieldName}. It does not move them.
fire: {defender}'s {shieldName} catches the worst of the {attack}. The flame finds no path past it.
Heat from the {attack} washes over {defender}'s {shieldName}. {defender} stays cool.
{defender} angles {defender}'s {shieldName} into the {attack}. The shield scorches. {defender} does not.
cold: Ice from the {attack} forms across {defender}'s {shieldName} — and stops there.
{defender} catches the {attack} on {defender}'s {shieldName}. The cold never reaches them.
Frost from the {attack} crawls across {defender}'s {shieldName} and no further.
lightning: The {attack}'s lightning grounds out across {defender}'s {shieldName} in a crackling arc.
{defender}'s {shieldName} takes the {attack}. {defender} feels the tingle. Nothing more.
The {attack} finds {defender}'s {shieldName} and expends itself entirely.
acid: Acid from the {attack} splashes across {defender}'s {shieldName}, eating the surface. It stops there.
{defender}'s {shieldName} takes the {attack} on the chin. {defender} doesn't.
The {attack}'s corrosive spray is absorbed by {defender}'s {shieldName}. It looks worse for wear. {defender} does not.
force: Invisible force from the {attack} hammers {defender}'s {shieldName} — transmitted to {defender}'s arm. They hold.
{defender} braces {defender}'s {shieldName} against the {attack}. Braced things tend to hold.
The {attack}'s raw force hits {defender}'s {shieldName} and goes nowhere useful.

default: An invisible barrier surges around {defender} — {spellName} swallows the {attack} entirely.
{spellName} flares between {attacker} and {defender}. The {attack} never arrives.
Magical protection intercepts the {attack} before it can land. {spellName} earns its keep.
{defender}'s {spellName} shimmers into visibility as it absorbs the {attack}, then vanishes again.
piercing: The {attack} strikes an invisible wall — {spellName} — and stops dead.
{spellName} stops the {attack} as surely as any physical armor. More so, perhaps.
The {attack} meets magical resistance it cannot overcome. {spellName} holds.
slashing: The {attack} sweeps toward {defender} and hits nothing it can cut. {spellName} sees to that.
A sharp {attack} against something with no surface to cut. {spellName} dissipates it.
{spellName} gives the {attack} nothing to bite into. It is simply negated.
bludgeoning: The force of the {attack} disperses harmlessly against {spellName}'s invisible surface.
{spellName} absorbs the {attack}'s concussive impact with eerie silence.
The {attack}'s force meets magical resistance. The force doesn't win.
fire: The {attack} roars toward {defender} — {spellName} smothers it before it arrives.
The {attack} pushes against {spellName}'s barrier and cannot push through.
Magical protection and fire have a long-standing disagreement. Today, {spellName} wins.
cold: The {attack} is intercepted by {spellName}. {defender} feels nothing.
The {attack} crashes against {spellName}'s barrier and shatters into harmless crystals.
{spellName} keeps the {attack}'s chill at arm's length — literally.
lightning: The {attack}'s lightning strikes {spellName}'s barrier and discharges harmlessly.
The {attack} forks and dissipates against {spellName}'s magical field.
The {attack}'s electrical energy is grounded entirely within {spellName}'s protective radius.
thunder: The {attack}'s shockwave hammers {spellName} and finds itself absorbed without trace.
The {attack}'s force breaks against {spellName}'s magic like a wave against stone.
{spellName} quietly dismantles the {attack} before {defender} even feels it.
necrotic: The {attack}'s death energy washes against {spellName}'s barrier — and finds it unwilling to yield.
The {attack}'s necrotic force cannot unravel what {spellName} has woven. {defender} is safe.
{spellName} stands between the {attack} and {defender}. It is enough.
radiant: Radiant light from the {attack} flares brilliantly against {spellName}'s field — beautiful and harmless.
{spellName} redirects the {attack}'s divine energy away from {defender} without effort.
Holy {attack} meets {spellName}'s magical protection. Neither yields. {defender} takes no damage.
force: The {attack} hammers {spellName}'s invisible barrier and expends itself entirely.
Even the {attack}'s raw arcane force cannot breach {spellName}'s protection. Not today.
psychic: {spellName} extends its protection to the mental realm — the {attack} finds no entry.
Magical warding bends the {attack} away from {defender}'s mind.
The {attack} rushes in and finds {spellName} already waiting for it.

default: The {attack} scrapes across {defender}'s bark-hardened skin — {spellName} holds firm.
{spellName} has made {defender}'s skin as tough as old wood. The {attack} proves it.
The {attack} lands against what feels like hitting a tree. That's because it is. {spellName}.
Bark-skin courtesy of {spellName} — {attacker}'s {attack} might as well have struck an oak.
piercing: The {attack} sinks into bark-hardened skin and stops. {spellName} did its job.
{spellName}'s protection is exactly as thick as it needs to be. The {attack} proves it.
Bark doesn't give way for the {attack}. {spellName} has made {defender} bark.
slashing: The {attack} rakes across hardened bark-skin and finds no purchase. {spellName} holds.
The {attack} against {defender}'s {spellName}-hardened skin is like trying to slash a tree. It doesn't work.
{spellName} has replaced vulnerable skin with something far less cuttable by the {attack}.
bludgeoning: Bark absorbs the {attack} as well as any armor. {spellName} handles it.
Dense bark-hardened skin distributes the {attack}'s blunt force harmlessly.
Hammering bark doesn't hurt the tree. {defender} has become the tree, courtesy of {spellName}.
fire: Bark is flammable, but {spellName}'s protection is thicker than the {attack} is hot. Today.
The {attack} chars the bark surface created by {spellName}. It goes no deeper.
Bark burns — slowly. The {attack} doesn't stay long enough. {spellName} holds.
acid: The {attack}'s acid eats into bark more slowly than flesh. {spellName} buys enough time.
{spellName} has made {defender}'s skin acid-resistant by virtue of simply being bark.
The {attack} works on the bark created by {spellName} — and runs out before it runs out of bark.

default: {defender} is protected by forces unseen. The {attack} falls just short.
Something stops the {attack} that {attacker} can't quite account for.
{defender} shrugs off the {attack} through means {attacker} can't easily explain.
Protected by... something. {attacker}'s {attack} proves insufficient either way.
piercing: The {attack} finds no way through — some protection {attacker} hadn't anticipated.
An unseen force deflects the {attack} at the last moment.
{defender} is better protected than {attacker} could have known.
slashing: The {attack} meets unseen resistance and deflects without purchase.
Some unknown protection sheds the {attack} without explanation.
{attacker}'s {attack} is turned aside by something they never saw.
bludgeoning: The {attack} lands — and the impact goes nowhere. Unclear why.
Something absorbs the {attack}'s force before it can do damage.
{defender} doesn't react to the {attack} the way {attacker} expected. More protected than apparent.
magical: Magical protections layer around {defender} — {attacker}'s {attack} finds no gap.
Arcane wards intercept the {attack} in ways {attacker} can't easily counter.
{defender} is wrapped in more magical protection than is immediately obvious.
